title: Xerisced Glassy-Jelly Citrusy Peruvian Serrano Ham Tagine
description: This dish is a flavorful and healthy meal-prep option that combines the sweetness of citrus and the spiciness of serrano ham. It's perfect for a weeknight dinner or a special occasion.
instructions: Heat the oil in a clay tagine over medium heat.
Add the sliced red onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the sliced ginger and cook for another minute.
Add the serrano ham and cook for 2-3 minutes, until lightly browned.
Add the lime juice, orange juice, grapefruit juice, and honey to the tagine. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the cornstarch to thicken the sauce.
Add the chopped cilantro and crushed red pepper to the tagine. Stir to combine.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Serve over rice or with bread, if desired.
